title: cordyla pinnata
intro: Cordyla pinnata, also known as the keruing tree or takis, is a type of tree native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia, Malaysia, and Thailand. The medicinal properties of Cordyla pinnata lie in its bark, which contains compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cordyla pinnata are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, including skin and respiratory infections. Cordyla pinnata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
